text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Raymond Kowalski
MRN: [REDACTED]
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Attending Oncologist, Medical Oncology]
PERFORMING RADIOLOGIST: [Attending Interventional Radiologist]
CLINICAL DIAGNOSIS / HISTORY: 71-year-old male with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC), Gleason score 9 (Grade Group 5), known germline BRCA2 pathogenic variant (c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22) with confirmed somatic biallelic loss. Prior therapies: leuprolide (ongoing), abiraterone (discontinued, progression), enzalutamide (discontinued, progression), olaparib 200 mg BID (ongoing at time of biopsy). Currently with rising PSA (14.6 ng/mL, up from nadir of 2.1 ng/mL) and new hepatic and pulmonary lesions identified on restaging CT. Biopsy performed for histologic characterization of hepatic metastases and evaluation of potential treatment-emergent neuroendocrine transdifferentiation, as well as tissue acquisition for molecular profiling.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN INFORMATION

Specimen Designation: A — Liver, right hepatic lobe, CT-guided core needle biopsy
Type of Specimen: CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy (18-gauge, 3 cores submitted)
Date of Procedure: [DATE REDACTED — TO BE POPULATED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
Clinical Indication: Evaluation of new hepatic metastases; rule out treatment-emergent neuroendocrine prostate cancer; tissue for molecular profiling
Fixative: 10% neutral buffered formalin

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

GROSS DESCRIPTION

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled with patient name and "liver biopsy." Three tan-white, cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments are present, measuring 1.2 cm x 0.1 cm, 1.0 cm x 0.1 cm, and 0.8 cm x 0.1 cm in greatest dimensions. The tissue is firm to rubbery in consistency. All three cores are submitted entirely in one cassette (A1). Sections are cut and stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Representative sections are submitted for immunohistochemical analysis per protocol.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION

All three cores are examined. Sections show fragments of liver parenchyma with near-total replacement by a malignant neoplasm. The tumor is composed predominantly of glands and nests of cells with moderate amounts of amphophilic cytoplasm, enlarged hyperchromatic nuclei with prominent nucleoli, and moderate nuclear pleomorphism. The glandular architecture is consistent with adenocarcinoma. Infiltrating tumor cells are present within portal tracts and sinusoidal spaces, and focal desmoplastic stromal reaction is present at the tumor-liver interface.

In addition to the predominant glandular component, a subpopulation of tumor cells with morphologic features suggestive of neuroendocrine differentiation is identified. These cells display a more monotonous appearance with round to oval nuclei, finely stippled ("salt and pepper") chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli, and scant cytoplasm. They are arranged in small nests, trabeculae, and single-file patterns interspersed within and at the periphery of the glandular component, comprising an estimated 10-15% of the overall tumor cellularity. No well-formed organoid or rosette architecture diagnostic of small cell carcinoma is identified; the morphology is more consistent with focal/mixed neuroendocrine differentiation rather than overt small cell/large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma at this time.

Mitotic figures are numerous throughout the specimen (estimated >10 per 10 high-power fields in the most active areas). Apoptotic bodies are readily identified. No definitive lymphovascular invasion is identified within the sampled cores, though the biopsy nature limits evaluation. No perineural invasion is identified within the hepatic parenchyma.

The background non-neoplastic liver parenchyma is largely replaced by tumor. The residual hepatocytes show mild cytoplasmic vacuolization without significant steatosis, cholestasis, or bridging fibrosis.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDIES

Immunoperoxidase staining was performed on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue using standard protocols with appropriate positive and negative controls.

PROSTATIC LINEAGE MARKERS:
- PSA (Prostate-Specific Antigen): Positive, moderate to strong cytoplasmic and luminal staining in the glandular component; weak to absent in the neuroendocrine-differentiated component.
- NKX3.1: Positive, strong nuclear staining in the adenocarcinoma component; weak/focal in the neuroendocrine component.
- PSMA (Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen): Positive, strong membranous staining in the glandular component.

ANDROGEN RECEPTOR:
- AR (Androgen Receptor): Positive, moderate nuclear staining (scored 2+, estimated 60-70% of tumor nuclei in the adenocarcinoma component). Weak/focal in the neuroendocrine component (approximately 20-30% of neuroendocrine cells).

NEUROENDOCRINE MARKERS:
- Synaptophysin: Focally positive — granular cytoplasmic staining identified in approximately 10-15% of tumor cells, predominantly in the morphologically distinct neuroendocrine population and in scattered glandular cells.
- Chromogranin A: Focally positive — cytoplasmic staining in approximately 10% of tumor cells, corroborating neuroendocrine differentiation in a subset of cells.
- CD56 (NCAM): Focally positive in cells corresponding to the neuroendocrine component.
- INSM1 (Insulinoma-Associated Protein 1): Focally positive, nuclear staining in the neuroendocrine component (approximately 10-15% of cells).

CELL CYCLE / PROLIFERATION:
- Ki-67 (MIB-1): High proliferative index, approximately 55% of tumor cell nuclei staining positive. Proliferative activity is elevated in both the glandular and neuroendocrine components.

OTHER MARKERS:
- CK7: Negative in the majority of tumor cells (rare weak focal positivity in glandular component).
- CK20: Negative.
- TTF-1: Negative (excludes primary lung adenocarcinoma or small cell carcinoma of pulmonary origin).
- CDX2: Negative.
- GATA3: Negative.
- p53: Aberrant/mutant pattern — strong diffuse nuclear overexpression in >80% of tumor cells (consistent with TP53 gain-of-function mutation, correlating with known TP53 p.R248W).
- PTEN: Loss of expression (complete absence of cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells with intact internal endothelial controls), consistent with biallelic PTEN deletion.
- RB1: Markedly reduced/absent nuclear expression in the majority of tumor cells (consistent with RB1 loss, which is associated with neuroendocrine transdifferentiation).

FLUORESCENCE IN SITU HYBRIDIZATION (FISH):
- BRCA2 (13q12.3): Homozygous deletion confirmed. No residual BRCA2 signal identified in tumor nuclei. FISH result consistent with biallelic BRCA2 loss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ANCILLARY STUDIES — PENDING

The following studies have been submitted and results are PENDING at the time of this report. An addendum will be issued upon completion:

1. Tissue submitted for next-generation sequencing (NGS) molecular profiling panel — specimen submitted to [Molecular Pathology Laboratory]. Expected turnaround: 10-14 business days.
2. E-cadherin immunostain — ordered to further characterize epithelial-mesenchymal transition features; result pending.
3. AURKA (Aurora Kinase A) FISH — ordered per neuroendocrine differentiation protocol; result pending.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS

SPECIMEN A — LIVER, RIGHT HEPATIC LOBE, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

METASTATIC PROSTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA WITH FOCAL NEUROENDOCRINE DIFFERENTIATION (MIXED HISTOLOGY), CONSISTENT WITH TREATMENT-EMERGENT NEUROENDOCRINE PROSTATE CANCER (t-NEPC) FEATURES.

Grade/Histology: Gleason Grade Group 5 adenocarcinoma pattern (Gleason 9-10 equivalent morphology in metastatic context), with focal (10-15%) neuroendocrine transdifferentiation.
Proliferative index (Ki-67): 55% (high).
Prostate origin confirmed: PSA positive, NKX3.1 positive, PSMA positive.
Androgen receptor: Retained, moderate expression (2+).
Neuroendocrine markers: Synaptophysin and chromogranin A focally positive (10-15% of tumor cells); CD56 focally positive; INSM1 focally positive — supporting partial neuroendocrine transdifferentiation.
BRCA2 status by FISH: Homozygous deletion confirmed (biallelic loss).
TP53: Mutant pattern by IHC (diffuse strong overexpression, consistent with known p.R248W).
PTEN: Loss of expression by IHC (consistent with homozygous PTEN deletion).
RB1: Markedly reduced/absent expression by IHC (consistent with RB1 biallelic inactivation, a known driver of neuroendocrine transdifferentiation).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

COMMENT

This liver core needle biopsy demonstrates metastatic prostatic adenocarcinoma with focal features of treatment-emergent neuroendocrine prostate cancer (t-NEPC). The combined adenocarcinoma and neuroendocrine components, retained PSA and NKX3.1 expression (confirming prostatic lineage), high Ki-67 proliferative index of 55%, RB1 loss, and the clinical context of prior AR-targeted therapy (abiraterone and enzalutamide) and PARP inhibitor therapy (olaparib) are collectively consistent with treatment-related lineage plasticity and early neuroendocrine transdifferentiation. The morphology does not fulfill criteria for overt small cell carcinoma at this time; the picture is best characterized as a mixed adenocarcinoma/focally neuroendocrine-differentiated phenotype.

Loss of RB1 expression in this setting is particularly notable, as RB1 loss in combination with TP53 mutation is well recognized as a critical driver of neuroendocrine lineage plasticity in prostate cancer. The retained AR expression (moderate, 2+) and weak PSA expression in the neuroendocrine component suggests an intermediate/transitional state rather than complete lineage switch.

Molecular profiling results are pending (see Ancillary Studies). These results will be reported in a separate addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DIAGNOSING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Surgical Pathologist, Department of Pathology]
SIGN-OUT DATE: [DATE REDACTED — TO BE POPULATED PROGRAMMATICALLY]

This report has been reviewed and electronically signed by the attending pathologist of record.

*** PRELIMINARY REPORT — ADDENDUM TO FOLLOW UPON COMPLETION OF PENDING STUDIES ***
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
